text: This exercise is ring roller coaster one. It is one of several ring roller coaster exercises featured in our curriculum and focuses primarily on robotic wrist articulation.
Two rings are maneuvered around a metal pipe, navigating a complex and winding path from one end to the other.

In our experience, we have found that the best way to quickly move the rings is by using only the right hand, as it allows for maximal range of motion while simultaneously moving the ring in the right to left direction.
The participant uses their right hand, pronated, to grasp the ring with a straight grasper, optimizing the range of motion for navigating the ring along the course.
The ring travels over the horizontal or upper curves of the metal pipe, with the hand supinated. As the ring moves upwards, the hand is pronated, and as it moves downwards, the hand is supinated.
The surgeon manipulates the second ring along the metal pipe, using pronation and supination of the hand to navigate the ring through the curves and complete the exercise.

Keys to scoring high on this exercise require minimizing touching the pipe and excessive force, avoiding dropping the rings, maintaining good vision, and moving efficiently through the course as time is against you.
